name: Green Chemistry Process Design
description: Green chemistry redesigns a synthesis around its twelve principles: a candidate route is scored for atom economy, safer solvents and reagents are chosen, and the E-factor (mass of waste per mass of product) is estimated; the route is iterated with catalysis and waste reduction until both metrics are acceptable.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Target product"]
    N2["Design synthesis route"]
    N3["Compute atom economy"]
    N4{"High atom economy?"}
    N5["Redesign route"]
    N6["Choose safer solvent/reagents"]
    N7["Estimate E-factor"]
    N8{"E-factor acceptable?"}
    N9["Reduce waste / catalysis"]
    N10["Green process"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|redesign| N2
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|improve| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The atom economy - a search for synthetic efficiency
The E factor 25 years on: the rise of green chemistry and sustainability
